steps.title: Recall the infinite-solutions condition
steps.content: Two linear equations have infinitely many solutions only when they describe the same line — identical slope and identical $y$-intercept.

steps.title: Match the equations
steps.content:
Given line: $y = \tfrac{2}{7}x + 3$. The second line is $y = mx + b$, so we need $m = \tfrac{2}{7}$ and $b = 3$.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
